title: WindowsNativeJailAdapter implementation (wraps audited Rust crate from MT-045 decision)
goal: Implement the WindowsNativeJailAdapter per spec §3.5.2: Win32 Job Objects + AppContainer + Restricted Tokens, wrapping the Rust crate chosen by MT-045's license audit. Strict isolation with Win32-native fidelity (registry isolation, native DirectX path, no WSL2 round-trip). Selected when ProcessSpec.required_capabilities includes win32_native_fidelity per spec §3.5.3.
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/sandbox/windows_native_jail/{mod.rs, adapter.rs, capabilities.rs, job_object_wrap.rs}; integration tests under src/backend/handshake_core/tests/windows_native_jail_adapter_tests.rs (gated by `#[cfg(target_os="windows")]` and `#[cfg_attr(not(feature="win-native-integration"), ignore)]`). Cargo.toml additions: the audited crate from MT-045 decision record (codex-windows-sandbox OR rappct), windows-rs (already a workspace dep likely; verify), winapi only if the audited crate requires it transitively.
unblock_conditions: MT-045 decision record exists at .GOV/roles_shared/records/sandbox/WP-KERNEL-004-windows-native-jail-crate-license-audit.json with decision.chosen_crate != null AND operator_escalation_required == false. If escalation required, MT-046 stays BLOCKED.
implementation_notes: Pre-step: implementer reads the MT-045 decision record, extracts decision.chosen_crate and decision.chosen_version, and adds that exact crate+version to Cargo.toml as a workspace dependency. DO NOT hand-roll Win32 Job Object / AppContainer / Restricted Token code (spec §3.5.2: 'DO NOT hand-roll Win32 isolation' + OpenAI Codex 2026 pattern). adapter.rs implements SandboxAdapter by delegating to the chosen crate: (1) `spawn`: construct the crate's sandbox-spec from ProcessSpec (map ProcessSpec.cmd -> argv, env -> environment block, binds -> per-AppContainer file-grant API call, resource_limits -> Job Object accounting limits via the crate's API, net_policy -> Windows Filtering Platform rules via the crate IF supported else SpawnFailed with reason); the spawned process becomes a child of a Job Object so kill+orphan-reclaim are guaranteed by the OS; capture the crate's process handle as `sandbox_internal_id`; mint ProcessHandle with UUID v7. (2) `exec`: many Win32 jails do NOT support exec-after-spawn (the AppContainer SID is process-scoped) — if the crate does not support exec, return SandboxAdapterError::SpawnFailed{ reason: "WindowsNativeJailAdapter does not support exec; declare cmd in ProcessSpec" } loud per spec §3.5.3. (3) `fs_bind`: post-spawn bind = unsupported on AppContainer (same reason); SpawnFailed loud. (4) `net_policy`: post-spawn change uses WFP rule update IF the crate supports it. (5) `kill`: TerminateJobObject for Term/Kill; map Int to GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent CTRL_C_EVENT. (6) `status`: GetExitCodeProcess + IsProcessInJob. (7) `exit_code`: GetExitCodeProcess. (8) `capabilities()`: returns AdapterCapabilities { adapter_id: "windows_native_jail", filesystem_isolation_strength: VeryStrong (AppContainer file-grant model is per-SID), network_isolation_strength: Strong (WFP, but per-process granularity depends on crate), gpu_passthrough: VendorAgnostic (DirectX/Direct3D pass via host driver), stdio_throughput_class: Medium (Win32 anonymous pipes), win32_native_fidelity: true, cross_machine_portable: false (Windows-only) }. capabilities.rs centralizes the AdapterCapabilities so MT-048 IPC reads from one place. job_object_wrap.rs is a thin wrapper around the chosen crate's Job-Object handle type so the rest of the module never imports the crate directly (insulation layer; if MT-045 chose a different crate in a future revision, only job_object_wrap.rs changes). Integration tests (gated): spawn `cmd.exe /c echo hello`, assert exit code 0 + stdout=="hello\r\n"; spawn a process that tries to write outside its AppContainer-granted paths and assert ACCESS_DENIED; spawn a process that tries to open a TCP socket and assert WFP blocks it (when net_policy=DenyAll). Cross-platform CI: tests are `#[cfg(target_os="windows")]` so non-Windows CI skips; presence of a `#[cfg(not(target_os="windows"))]` smoke that asserts SandboxAdapterError::AdapterUnavailable so callers on non-Windows hosts get a typed error rather than a build failure.
proof_commands: cargo test -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target windows_native_jail_adapter_tests --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration
risk_if_missed: Without the strict-isolation adapter, jobs declaring required_capabilities=win32_native_fidelity have no satisfier and selection (MT-043) fails loud; certain Win32-native workloads (registry-touching plugin processes, DirectX-dependent helpers) cannot be sandboxed.
validator_focus: Adapter delegates to the MT-045-chosen crate (no hand-rolled Job Object code in repo); capabilities().win32_native_fidelity==true; capabilities().cross_machine_portable==false; AccessDenied is the integration-test assertion for outside-AppContainer file writes; non-Windows host returns AdapterUnavailable not a build failure.

minimum_controls: Grep test asserts NO direct windows-rs Job Object or AppContainer API call appears outside job_object_wrap.rs (forces the insulation layer per spec §3.5.2 'DO NOT hand-roll').
Non-Windows host test asserts the adapter constructs and returns AdapterUnavailable typed error rather than panicking or refusing to compile.
Integration test asserts a process inside the jail cannot read C:\Windows\System32 unless explicitly granted (proves AppContainer SID restriction is wired).
